title: Lecture Three Edge-Mode Symbols
summary: This notation map fixes the symbols used in Lecture Three for the parity-anomaly half level, the sign-changing mass profile, and the boundary-localized Dirac mode.
assumptions: The boundary is taken at x_1 = 0.
The bulk Dirac fermions are described in 2+1 dimensions.
regime: Lecture Three continuum edge-mode and parity-anomaly discussion.
scope: Shared notation for the domain-wall zero-mode and anomaly-inflow branch.

source_anchors.notes: Core notation for the half-level parity anomaly and the chiral boundary mode.

representation: Shared symbol table for the continuum edge-mode proof family.

symbols.meaning: Dirac mass whose sign controls the half-level and the wall profile
boundary-localized spinor solving the reduced 1+1-dimensional Dirac equation
normal gamma matrix whose eigenvalue fixes the boundary chirality
Dirac operator tangent to the boundary

retrieval_hints: Use before answering a Lecture Three edge-mode question that mixes bulk parity anomaly, wall profile, and boundary chirality.
